schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Companies Act Valuation — Section 247
schemas.description: Companies Act valuation by IBBI Registered Valuer. Section 247 reports. Preferential allotment (Section 62), buyback (Section 68), amalgamation (Sections 230-232), demerger, share swap.

mainEntity.name: Is an IBBI Registered Valuer mandatory for Companies Act valuations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Section 247 mandates that any valuation required under the Companies Act must be performed by a Registered Valuer registered under IBBI Regulations, 2017. A generic CA certificate is not sufficient for these purposes.

mainEntity.name: What is a swap ratio valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: In a merger or amalgamation, the swap ratio determines how many shares of the surviving company each shareholder of the merging company receives. It is based on the relative fair values of both companies. The Registered Valuer certifies the fairness of this ratio.

mainEntity.name: Can a Company Secretary issue a valuation report under Companies Act?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Only a Registered Valuer registered with IBBI under the relevant asset class can issue valuation reports required under the Companies Act, 2013. Company Secretaries handle compliance filings, not valuations.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for non-compliance with Section 247?

acceptedAnswer.text: If a company makes a valuation without appointing a Registered Valuer where required, the company and every officer in default can face penalties. The valuation itself may be challenged before NCLT.

mainEntity.name: Does Virtual Auditor provide valuation reports for NCLT proceedings?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. As an IBBI Registered Valuer, CA V. Viswanathan provides valuation reports for NCLT-filed schemes of arrangement, oppression/mismanagement matters, and IBC proceedings. Reports are formatted per NCLT requirements.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost of a Companies Act valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 247 valuation from \u20b950,000 for a single entity. Swap ratio valuations for mergers from \u20b91,50,000. Buyback valuation from \u20b940,000. Contact Virtual Auditor at +91 99622 60333 for a specific quote based on transaction complexity.
